title: alchemilla vulgaris var. fissa
intro: Alchemilla vulgaris var. fissa, also known as lady's mantle, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the rose family (Rosaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids, tannins, and organic acids. The medicinal properties of lady's mantle have been reported to help alleviate menstrual cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome, while also exhibiting wound-healing and antiseptic properties. Lady's mantle can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support women's health and alleviate digestive issues.
